tests.description: Populating demo data succeeds without errors and creates expected entities
steps: Create a new organization via the registration/onboarding flow
When prompted, choose to populate demo data
Wait for demo data population to complete
Verify no error toasts or failure messages appear
tests.setup: Ensure the dev environment is running with a clean database. Delete any existing demo org or create a fresh user account for testing.
tests.expected: Demo data populates successfully with no errors (previously failed with if_entries neighbor_host_id FK constraint — fixed by deferring AP neighbor via NeighborUpdate). The dashboard shows networks and hosts.

tests.description: Headquarters network has ~30 hosts across multiple subnets with a complex topology
steps: Navigate to Networks and select the 'Headquarters' network
Open the topology view for Headquarters
Verify hosts are organized across distinct subnets (Management, Office LAN, Servers, Storage, IoT, Docker Bridge, Guest WiFi)
Verify approximately 30 hosts are visible
Check that the topology looks interesting and well-connected (not just a flat list)
tests.expected: Headquarters topology shows ~30 hosts across 7 subnets with a rich, interconnected layout including firewalls, switches, hypervisors, Docker hosts, workstations, and IoT devices.

tests.description: Data Center network has ~20 hosts with a different topology character than HQ
steps: Navigate to Networks and select the 'Data Center' network
Open the topology view for Data Center
Verify hosts are organized across distinct subnets (Management, Compute, Storage, DMZ, Docker Bridge, VPN Tunnel)
Verify approximately 20 hosts are visible
Compare to HQ topology — it should have a different character (more focused on compute/storage workloads)
tests.expected: Data Center topology shows ~20 hosts across 6 subnets with a distinct layout from HQ, featuring load balancers, app servers, hypervisor with VMs, monitoring containers, and storage nodes.

tests.description: HQ Proxmox hypervisors show HostVirtualization edges to their VMs in the topology
steps: Open the Headquarters topology view
Locate proxmox-hv01 and proxmox-hv02 hosts
Verify that virtualization edges connect proxmox-hv01 to its VMs (gitlab-vm, nextcloud-vm)
Verify that virtualization edges connect proxmox-hv02 to its VMs (keycloak-vm, db-vm)
Check that the edge style/color distinguishes virtualization from other edge types
tests.expected: HostVirtualization edges are visible between Proxmox hypervisors and their respective VMs. The VMs are visually connected to their hypervisor hosts.

tests.description: DC Proxmox hypervisor shows HostVirtualization edges to its VMs in the topology
steps: Open the Data Center topology view
Locate dc-proxmox-hv01 host
Verify that virtualization edges connect dc-proxmox-hv01 to its VMs (argocd-vm, graylog-vm, mariadb-vm)
Check that the edge style/color distinguishes virtualization from other edge types
tests.expected: HostVirtualization edges are visible between the DC Proxmox hypervisor and its 3 VMs.

tests.description: HQ Docker host shows ServiceVirtualization edges to container services
steps: Open the Headquarters topology view
Locate docker-prod01 host
Verify that container service edges connect Docker daemon to containers (traefik, vaultwarden, gitea, mailcow)
Click on docker-prod01 to inspect its services — verify both Docker daemon and Portainer are listed
Verify the container services appear on the Docker Bridge subnet
tests.expected: ServiceVirtualization edges connect the Docker daemon service to 4 container services. Container services are visible on the Docker Bridge subnet.

tests.description: DC Docker host shows ServiceVirtualization edges to container services
steps: Open the Data Center topology view
Locate dc-docker01 host
Verify that container service edges connect Docker daemon to containers (prometheus, grafana, jaeger, loki)
Verify the container services appear on the DC Docker Bridge subnet
tests.expected: ServiceVirtualization edges connect the DC Docker daemon service to 4 container services on the Docker Bridge subnet.

tests.description: HQ core switch shows LLDP/CDP physical link edges to connected devices
steps: Open the Headquarters topology view
Locate unifi-usw-48 (core switch)
Verify physical link edges connect the switch to neighboring devices (pfsense-fw01, truenas-primary, proxmox-hv01, proxmox-hv02, docker-prod01, unifi-ap-lobby)
Verify edges are bidirectional where expected
tests.expected: PhysicalLink edges are visible between the HQ core switch and its LLDP neighbors, showing the physical network topology.

tests.description: DC switch shows LLDP/CDP physical link edges to connected devices
steps: Open the Data Center topology view
Locate dc-switch-01
Verify physical link edges connect the switch to neighboring devices (dc-fw01, dc-proxmox-hv01, dc-docker01, haproxy-lb01)
Verify edges are bidirectional where expected
tests.expected: PhysicalLink edges are visible between the DC switch and its LLDP neighbors.

tests.description: HQ network has 3 groups defining service flow relationships
steps: Open the Headquarters topology view
Look for group overlays or navigate to the groups section
Verify 'Monitoring Stack' group exists (HubAndSpoke: Prometheus -> Grafana, Uptime Kuma)
Verify 'Backup Flow' group exists (RequestPath: Proxmox VE -> TrueNAS)
Verify 'Network Access Path' group exists (RequestPath: pfSense -> Portainer)
Check that group edges have distinct colors and styles
tests.expected: 3 groups are visible on the HQ topology with correct member services, edge types (HubAndSpoke/RequestPath), and distinct visual styling.

tests.description: DC network has 3 groups defining service flow relationships
steps: Open the Data Center topology view
Look for group overlays or navigate to the groups section
Verify 'Web Traffic Flow' group exists (RequestPath: HAProxy -> Web Application -> RabbitMQ)
Verify 'Observability Stack' group exists (HubAndSpoke: Prometheus -> Grafana, Jaeger)
Verify 'Storage Tier' group exists (HubAndSpoke: MinIO -> Ceph, Elasticsearch)
Check that group edges have distinct colors and styles
tests.expected: 3 groups are visible on the DC topology with correct member services, edge types, and distinct visual styling.

tests.description: Tags and SNMP credentials are present and distributed across both networks
steps: Navigate to the Tags section
Verify 10 tags exist and are applied to hosts across both networks
Navigate to Credentials/SNMP section
Verify 2 SNMP credentials exist
tests.expected: 10 tags distributed across both networks and 2 SNMP credentials are present.

tests.description: A public share exists for the HQ topology
steps: Navigate to Shares section
Verify a public share exists for the Headquarters network topology
Open the public share link in an incognito/private browser window
Verify the topology is viewable without authentication
tests.expected: A public share link works and displays the HQ topology to unauthenticated users.

tests.description: Both topologies look visually interesting and demonstrate the product's value
steps: View both network topologies side by side (or one after another)
Assess visual quality: Are the topologies rich and interesting?
Verify they look meaningfully different from each other
Check that virtualization relationships (VM edges, container edges) add visual depth
Verify the topologies would impress a potential customer seeing the demo for the first time
tests.expected: Both topologies are visually rich, distinct from each other, and effectively showcase virtualization relationships, physical links, and service groups. The demo feels polished and impressive.
